findings.issue: LoginPage.handleOAuthLogin and InvitationPage.handleOAuthLogin use an `if (loading) return` guard (R27) but the buttons also have `disabled={loading}`. The early-return is defensive, but the test suite never exercises the guard — clicking the same button twice in quick succession before React commits `disabled` is the very scenario the guard is meant to cover, and there is no test that verifies initOAuth is not called twice. As a result, regression risk for accidental double-init exists but is invisible to CI.
findings.remediation: Add a test that fires two clicks in the same tick and asserts initOAuth is called exactly once.

findings.issue: InvitationCompletePage success path schedules a 2000ms redirect via `setTimeout(() => window.location.assign(appBase), 2000)` (line 81). The success-path test (`redeems with stored verifier and accepts with URL invitation token`) asserts `screen.getByText('Invitation accepted!')` but never verifies that `window.location.assign` is called with the appBase URL, and never verifies that the timer cleanup runs on unmount. If the timer callback were removed or the redirect URL changed, the test would still pass.
findings.remediation: Add `vi.useFakeTimers()` to advance time 2000ms and assert `mockAssign` was called with the appBase URL; also assert the timer is cleared on unmount before the 2000ms elapses.

findings.issue: OAuthCallbackPage's integration with isSafeReturnTo is not tested end-to-end. isSafeReturnTo is tested in isolation (`rejects cross-origin URLs`, `rejects protocol-relative URLs`), but there is no test that renders the OAuthCallbackPage with `return_to=//evil.com` or `return_to=https://attacker.com` and asserts the user is redirected to `/nodes` instead. The 27-round fix list calls out `isSafeReturnTo untested for same-origin absolute URLs`, but the integration test in OAuthCallbackPage is also missing for the unsafe-return-to path.
findings.remediation: Add a test rendering `/oauth/callback?handoff_id=...&app_code=...&return_to=//evil.com` and assert `mockAssign` is called with `/nodes`, not with the cross-origin path.

findings.issue: OAuthCallbackPage clears `localStorage.access_token` on the `!handoffId || !appCode` branch (line 132) and on the missing-verifier branch (line 142). A user who is already signed in (has a valid access_token) and navigates to `/oauth/callback` without query params (e.g., from a bookmark or back button) loses their session. The error redirect to `/login?error=Missing OAuth parameters` then forces re-authentication. The aggressive clearing is consistent with the OAuth callback being a one-shot endpoint, but it is not distinguishable from an accidental visit; a defensive check that doesn't clear on the same-origin navigation case might preserve the session.
findings.remediation: Consider only clearing `access_token` when a verifier was previously stored (i.e., the user actually started an OAuth flow), or document the design decision explicitly.

findings.issue: LoginPage declares `useEffect(() => { setError(searchParams.get('error')) }, [searchParams])` (line 33) on top of `useState<string | null>(searchParams.get('error'))` (line 31). On initial mount, both code paths read the same value, so the useEffect's first run is a no-op. The useEffect only matters if `searchParams` reference changes after mount. This is acknowledged as a code smell in the known-gaps list, but it is still dead code on the initial-render hot path and a source of confusion for future maintainers.
findings.remediation: Remove the useEffect; rely on the initial useState value plus a single useEffect (or just remove the useEffect if you don't expect the error param to change without a full unmount).
